items.title: Tracking Metrics
items.description: Metrics tell you how well a run performed; seeds and environment metadata tell you whether that run can be recreated. Log seed, library versions, and git commit alongside accuracy and loss — Tracking Metrics is the canonical home for what to measure and how to log it.

items.title: Versioning Experiments
items.description: Versioning captures code, data, and model artifacts over time. Reproducibility adds the runtime contract: same versioned artifacts + same seeds + same pinned environment should yield comparable results. Versioning Experiments owns artifact lineage; this topic owns the randomness and runtime pinning layer.

items.title: Stochastic Gradient Descent
items.description: SGD shuffles mini-batches and may sample stochastic gradients — both depend on RNG state. Fixing seeds stabilizes batch order and initialization so SGD comparisons reflect learning-rate choices, not shuffle luck. SGD owns the optimizer; this topic owns seeding the stochastic parts.

items.title: Cross-Validation
items.description: K-fold and stratified splits assign rows to folds using shuffles. Without random_state, fold membership changes and CV scores are not comparable across runs. Cross-Validation owns fold design; this topic owns why and how to fix the seed when creating folds.

items.title: Train / Val / Test Splits
items.description: Hold-out partitions use random shuffles (train_test_split). A logged random_state makes the same train/val/test rows appear on every rerun — essential before tuning hyperparameters fairly.
